mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in real-time retail sales signals resilient household demand and upward earnings revisions for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or has Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 5% and does not decline over 3 months, restrictive policy raises discount rates and slows credit-sensitive activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0% and 3-month change is >= 0
evidence_refs: fact:as of 2007-04-15 the policy-rate environment was restrictive relative to the prior expansion
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered mean returns are not below unconditional mean returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts contraction greater than 10% year over year signals weakening residential investment and future credit stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:the housing slowdown was observable before 2007-04-15 through released housing-starts data
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST contractions below -10% year over year are followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 mean returns or if the downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a flat or falling unemployment rate signals broad labor-income support without visible recession stress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-nonrising condition has 20-day forward returns no higher than periods failing the condition, or if interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 6-month trend in industrial production signals rising utilization and stronger goods-sector earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 6-month trends are not associated with higher subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative trends, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices during a non-easing funds-rate regime signals margin pressure and reduced odds of near-term policy relief, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS 3-month change >= 0
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-acceleration and non-easing interaction is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of cases or if the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid unemployment-rate increase signals rising recession risk and deterioration in expected earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts decline paired with a high federal funds rate signals that rate-sensitive demand is weakening before monetary relief arrives, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-decline and high-FEDFUNDS condition does not lower 5-day forward returns relative to either condition alone, or if triggered downside hit rate is below 52%.
